title: Context Assembly, Trust Labels, Token Budgets, Truncation, and Compaction · Guided Program Studio
summary: A public-interest product team incrementally builds budgeted provenance-aware context builder from original fixed provider, interaction, tool, approval, evaluation, and failure fixtures while retaining prior Python, HTTP, prompt, loop, security, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of budgeted provenance-aware context builder while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, change…
objectives: Label each context item by source, authority, tenant, purpose, timestamp, sensitivity, trust, instruction status, and expiry.
Reserve output and tool budget, measure or conservatively estimate input tokens, and admit context by task-specific value and risk.
Preserve active policy, user goal, approvals, tool schemas, recent results, unresolved errors, and evidence while removing redundant or expired material.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Context Source Contract
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop agent engineering team handles case 2189 during a controlled support-operations rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to assemble context by relevance, authority, provenance, recency, token budget, compaction loss tests, and explicit untrusted labels; loops, concurrency, tokens, time, retries, and spend stay below explicit budgets; then change model capability, API version, schema, or provider state and revalidate the adapter. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates.

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Compaction Regression
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-compaction-regression-iccotv" that uses case 6997 to assemble context by relevance, authority, provenance, recency, token budget, compaction loss tests, and explicit untrusted labels. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Compaction Regression.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Compaction Regression into observable performance evidence. For Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Compaction Regression, keep Compaction Regression connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-compaction-regression-iccotv
# Competency: agentpy-compaction-regression.
# Case 6997: a multilingual case-routing evaluation.
# Operating constraint: last valid checkpoint, audit trail, kill switch, and rollback path must survive failure.
# Changed case: inject cancellation, retry, repeated-call, checkpoint, or replay failure and recover without duplicate effects.
def evidence_agentpy_compaction_regression_iccotv(candidate_tokens: int = 18000, admitted_tokens: int = 12000, token_budget: int = 14000, trust_labeled_items: int = 42, unlabeled_items: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_iccotv = "iccotv-vtocci-6997"
    assert evidence_variant_iccotv
    evidence_axes_iccotv = (
        "authorityiccotv",
        "interaction6997",
        "toolvtocci",
        "stateiccotv6997",
        "evaluation6997iccotv",
        "recoveryvtocci6997",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_iccotv)) == 6
    if candidate_tokens < 1 or admitted_tokens < 0:
        return False, admitted_tokens, "context-count"
    if admitted_tokens > token_budget:
        return False, admitted_tokens, "context-budget"
    if trust_labeled_items < 1 or unlabeled_items > 0:
        return False, unlabeled_items, "trust-label-gap"
    if candidate_tokens < admitted_tokens:
        return False, candidate_tokens, "context-accounting"
    assert admitted_tokens <= token_budget and unlabeled_items == 0
    return True, admitted_tokens, "bounded-context-assembly"
